stem: A delivery truck travels at a constant speed on a highway. The equation d = 240 + 55t represents the total distance d, in miles, the truck has traveled t hours after beginning the highway portion of its route. What does the number 240 represent in this equation?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The distance, in miles, the truck had already traveled before entering the highway

choices.label: B
choices.text: The speed, in miles per hour, at which the truck travels on the highway

choices.label: C
choices.text: The total distance, in miles, the truck will travel on the highway

choices.label: D
choices.text: The time, in hours, the truck has been traveling

explanation: The equation d = 240 + 55t is in the form d = b + mt, where b is the y-intercept and m is the slope. When t = 0 (at the beginning of the highway portion), d = 240 + 55(0) = 240. This means the truck had already traveled 240 miles before starting the highway portion of its route. The constant term in a linear equation represents the initial value or starting point.

distractorRationale.A: Correct. The constant 240 represents the initial distance already traveled when t = 0.
distractorRationale.B: Student confuses the constant term with the coefficient of t. The speed is 55 miles per hour, represented by the coefficient of t.
distractorRationale.C: Student misinterprets the constant as a maximum or total rather than an initial value. The equation shows accumulating distance, not a limit.
distractorRationale.D: Student confuses the meaning of the constant with the variable. Time is represented by t, not by 240.
